title: Build Real Products
description: Every project must result in a working, testable, and deployable product — not a concept or prototype that stops at the demo stage.

title: Solve Real Problems
description: Engineering starts with understanding the problem domain deeply. Research the context, validate the need, then design the solution.

title: Combine Research with Engineering
description: Rigorous research methodology applied to engineering challenges. Literature review, hypothesis formation, experimentation, and validation.

title: Design Systems, Not Isolated Features
description: Individual features are meaningless without a coherent system. Architecture, interfaces, data flow, and integration are designed as a whole.

title: Focus on Deployable Outcomes
description: Engineering is complete when the system is deployed, monitored, and validated in the real world. Documentation and testing are part of the deliverable.

title: Continuous Improvement Through Testing
description: Every system is a hypothesis. Test, measure, iterate. Validation through real-world data is the only way to know if engineering decisions were correct.
